pattern: At each position, subtracting the largest usable prime that still keeps the array strictly increasing leaves the most room for every position after it.
hints: You may subtract at most one prime, strictly less than the current value, from each position -- or leave it untouched.
Sieve the primes up to 1000 once, then at every position figure out the smallest legal result you can leave behind that still beats the previous element.

To keep the array as 'low' as possible for future positions, greedily pick the largest valid prime to subtract; if even the largest usable prime cannot beat the previous element, the array can never be fixed.

edge_inputs.description: several elements each needing a different prime subtracted
already strictly increasing without needing any operation
two elements at the minimum value
the last element is too small to ever beat the one before it
repeated equal values must still be separated by a prime subtraction
value of 2 can never be reduced since no prime is smaller than it except itself is excluded
descending values that cannot be rescued by any subtraction

stress.description: long random arrays within the shared small value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
